assertions.description: INCORRECT: The analyzer detects R3 (220k) and C2 (1n) as a low-pass RC filter at 723 Hz. In the actual circuit, C2 is the MC34063AD timing capacitor (Ct pin) and R3 is part of the output voltage feedback divider. They share adjacent nets in the netlist due to topology but are not an intentional RC filter. The 0.05 Hz 'filter' R5+C4 is similarly spurious — C4 is the 3.3uF output bulk capacitor and R5 is the top resistor of the feedback divider.

R1 (10k to GND), R3 (220k), R4 (220k), and R5 (1M) form a series resistor voltage divider chain from the 180V output (J2) back to the MC34063AD feedback pin. This is the primary output regulation feedback network. The analyzer reports 0 voltage_dividers, missing this 4-resistor divider entirely.

Q1 is a BC557 PNP BJT and Q2 is an IRF740 N-channel power MOSFET. Together they form a composite external switch for the MC34063AD boost converter — Q1 drives Q2's gate via R7 (22 ohm gate resistor). The analyzer reports 0 transistor_circuits despite two transistors being present and connected in a meaningful circuit.

The subcircuit for U1 (MC34063AD) reports neighbor_components as an empty list []. In the actual circuit, U1 is closely connected to L1 (470uH boost inductor), D1 (LL4148 catch diode), D2 (UF4007 output rectifier), Q1 (BC557 gate driver), Q2 (IRF740 main switch), R2 (timing resistor), C3 (timing capacitor), and R6. The inductor and feedback_divider fields are also null despite L1 and R1/R3/R4/R5 being present and connected.

The resistor divider R1 (10k) + R3 (220k) + R4 (220k) + R5 (1M) from the 180V output to GND provides feedback to the MC34063AD's voltage comparator input. The analyzer reports 0 feedback_networks. This multi-resistor feedback divider pattern setting a high output voltage should ideally be detected as a feedback network associated with U1.
